lesson.title: Points & Lines
lesson.body: The **coordinate plane** has two perpendicular axes: the horizontal x-axis and vertical y-axis. Every point is described by an ordered pair (x, y) where x is the horizontal position and y is the vertical. The four **quadrants** are numbered I (top-right, +,+), II (top-left, −,+), III (bottom-left, −,−), and IV (bottom-right, +,−). A **line** is a set of infinitely many collinear points. Two points determine exactly one line.

rooms.question: In which quadrant is the point (−3, 5)?
rooms.explanation: x is negative and y is positive → Quadrant II (top-left region). Remember: Q I (+,+), Q II (−,+), Q III (−,−), Q IV (+,−). The y-axis separates negative x from positive x; the x-axis separates negative y from positive y.

rooms.question: Which point lies on the x-axis?
rooms.explanation: Points on the x-axis have y = 0. So (4, 0) lies on the x-axis. (0, 4) is on the y-axis. (4, 4) is in Quadrant I. (0, 0) is also on both axes, but (4, 0) is the clearest answer here.

rooms.question: What is the distance between the points (1, 3) and (4, 3)?
rooms.explanation: When two points share the same y-coordinate, the distance is simply |x₂ − x₁| = |4 − 1| = 3. The points are 3 units apart horizontally. The Pythagorean theorem isn't needed here because the movement is purely horizontal.

rooms.question: The midpoint of a segment from (2, 4) to (8, 10) is:
rooms.explanation: The midpoint formula averages the x-coordinates and averages the y-coordinates: M = ((2+8)/2, (4+10)/2) = (5, 7). The (10, 14) error sums without dividing. The (3, 5) error uses subtraction instead of addition.

rooms.question: Order these points from left to right (increasing x-coordinate):
rooms.explanation: Left to right means increasing x: −4 < −1 < 0 < 3. The y-coordinate is irrelevant when ordering by x position. Plotting these points would place them in a horizontal sequence from far left to right.

boss.name: The Cartesian Wraith
boss.taunt: X marks your grave. Y is obvious.

questions.question: Point A is at (−2, 5) and point B is at (4, −3). What are the coordinates of the midpoint of AB?
questions.explanation: M = ((−2+4)/2, (5+(−3))/2) = (2/2, 2/2) = (1, 1). Check that M is between A and B on both axes: x goes from −2 to 4, midpoint 1 ✓; y goes from 5 to −3, midpoint 1 ✓.

questions.question: A line passes through (0, 3) and (4, 3). Which statement is true?
answers: The line passes through the origin
questions.explanation: Both points have y = 3, so every point on the line has y = 3 — that's the equation of a horizontal line. A vertical line would require both x-coordinates to be equal. The slope of a horizontal line is 0, not 4.

questions.question: Three points are A(1, 2), B(3, 6), C(5, 10). Without graphing, determine if they are collinear.
answers: Yes — equal slopes between consecutive points
Yes — they all have even coordinates
No — three points can never be collinear
questions.explanation: Check slope A→B: (6−2)/(3−1) = 4/2 = 2. Slope B→C: (10−6)/(5−3) = 4/2 = 2. Equal slopes mean the same direction → all three are collinear. In fact, they all satisfy y = 2x: 2(1)=2 ✓, 2(3)=6 ✓, 2(5)=10 ✓.
